29.reason: Error: This modifies the string beyond its bounds, causing undefined behavior. The null terminator at b[v] is overwritten, and the string is no longer properly terminated.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This modifies the string beyond its bounds, causing undefined behavior. The null terminator at b[v] is overwritten, and the string is no longer properly terminated.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This modifies the string beyond its null terminator, causing undefined behavior. It also incorrectly adds a '+' at the end, which will be treated as an operator, leading to incorrect parsing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Writing beyond the declared size of array `b` which has size `len`; `v` can be up to `len-1`, so `b[v]` is out-of-bounds]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Writing beyond the declared size of array `b` (which is `len=100`) can cause buffer overflow; `v` is `strlen(b)` so `b[v]` is the null terminator position, overwriting it breaks string handling.]

66.reason: Error: Integer division may produce incorrect results due to order of operations; also, when g == 0, c[g-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Integer division may produce incorrect results due to order of operations; also, when g == 0, c[g-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Integer division order is wrong; should be d[g] / d[g+1] but the assignment overwrites d[g+1] incorrectly, and the logic for handling division in the presence of preceding operators is flawed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect operand order: should be `d[g] / d[g+1]` assigned to `d[g]`, not `d[g+1]`; also division by zero risk not handled but problem states valid input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect operand order: should be `d[g] / d[g+1]` but logic assumes left-to-right; however, more critically, if `d[g+1]` is zero, this causes division by zero. Also, the assignment overwrites `d[g+1]` but later addition/subtraction uses `d[0]` and `d[s+1]`, leading to incorrect accumulation.]
